metadata.title: Build the Pipeline, Part 1: Wire Up Your 3 Sources
metadata.playlist: Shop for Ingredients — Collect Your Madison Data
metadata.source: INFO 7375 Assignment 3 brief — Shop for Ingredients: Collect Your Madison Data

beats.narration_text: Hola — this is Liam. Episode 3 of 7. This is the one that earns forty points. The core of Assignment 3 is a single workflow with three different data sources. Not three URLs from the same website — three genuinely different kinds of sources. Today you wire the six-step skeleton and connect the first three nodes: an RSS feed, an API, and a CSV dataset. By the end you have three source nodes returning data you can look at. Episode 4 cleans and saves them. Let's go.

props.segment: Build the Pipeline, Part 1: Wire Up Your 3 Sources
props.command: Wire 3 source nodes into the 6-step skeleton: RSS + API + CSV dataset.
props.runningText: three different kinds of data, one workflow…
output: Step 1: the 6-step skeleton (Start → 3 sources → Cleanup → Save)
Step 2: RSS Read node — feed URL, no login
Step 3: HTTP Request node — API with key in credentials, not hard-coded

beats.narration_text: Before you add any data nodes, lay down the skeleton. The six steps are: Start, Source 1, Source 2, Source 3, Cleanup, and Save. The Start node is built into every new n8n workflow — it is already there when you open a blank canvas. Add three placeholder nodes after it, one for each source. Then add a Cleanup node and a Save node at the end. You will not fill in the node settings yet — you are just establishing the shape of the workflow. Wire them left to right: Start connects to Source 1, which connects to Source 2, which connects to Source 3, which connects to Cleanup, which connects to Save. This skeleton has a name for a reason: it shows you the order of operations before any real data flows through it. Look at the shape before you fill it. When the shape makes sense, start configuring the source nodes one at a time.

props.artifactHeading: Workflow skeleton — before any data flows
artifactLines: Start → Source 1 → Source 2 → Source 3 → Cleanup → Save
Start: built into every blank canvas — it's already there.
Source 1, 2, 3: placeholders — add them before configuring.
Cleanup: one node that merges, deduplicates, and normalizes the three streams.
Save: writes the clean output to a file on your computer.
Rule: establish the shape first. Configure nodes one at a time after the skeleton is wired.

beats.narration_text: Source 1 is an RSS feed — the easiest node to wire. In the node panel, search for RSS. You will find the RSS Read node. Drop it into the canvas in the Source 1 position. The only required field is the feed URL. Paste your RSS URL into the URL field. No login, no API key, no credentials — RSS feeds are public. Click Execute on just this node to see what it returns. You will get a list of items; each item is one article or post. Look at the fields: title, link, pubDate, content. Keep the fields that address your A2 problem. If the feed returns items with no dates or no titles, find a different feed — you cannot clean what is not there.

artifactLines: Search 'RSS' in the node panel → add 'RSS Read' node.
Set field: Feed URL — paste your RSS URL. No login required.
Execute this node alone: look at the output fields (title · link · pubDate · content).
Keep only the fields that address the A2 problem — ignore the rest.
If items have no dates or titles: find a different feed — you can't clean what isn't there.
props.sparkLine: RSS: URL in, items out. No login needed.

beats.narration_text: Source 2 is an API — and the most important rule for this node is: never paste your API key directly into the node configuration. n8n has a Credentials system for storing keys. Use it. Here is why: if you export the workflow JSON to submit it, the exported file should not contain your real API key — that would expose it. Search for HTTP Request in the node panel. In the node settings, set the URL to the API endpoint. For NewsAPI, the endpoint is newsapi.org/v2/everything — verify this against their current documentation. For YouTube Data API, the endpoint is googleapis.com/youtube/v3/search — verify this against the Google documentation. In both cases, store the API key in n8n credentials and reference the credential in the node settings, not in the URL. Execute this node alone and read the response. If you get an authorization error, the key is wrong or the credential is misconfigured — re-read the documentation for that API.

props.artifactHeading: API node — credentials, not hard-coded keys
artifactLines: Add 'HTTP Request' node → set the URL to the API endpoint.
NewsAPI endpoint: newsapi.org/v2/everything — verify against current docs.
YouTube Data API: googleapis.com/youtube/v3/search — verify against Google docs.
CRITICAL: store your API key in n8n Credentials — never paste it into the URL field.
Execute this node alone: auth error = key wrong or credential misconfigured.
Exported workflow JSON must NOT contain your real key — credentials solve this.
props.sparkLine: Credentials store the key. The URL uses the credential.

beats.narration_text: Source 3 is a dataset you downloaded from Kaggle or another Tier-1 source. n8n can read a CSV file directly with the Read Binary File node followed by a CSV parser, or — depending on your n8n version — a dedicated spreadsheet or CSV node. Search for 'CSV' or 'Spreadsheet' in the node panel to see what your version offers. Set the file path to wherever you saved the Kaggle CSV on your machine — use an absolute path, not a relative one. Execute this node alone and look at the output. You will see one row per record, with fields corresponding to the CSV headers. If the CSV has hundreds of fields, identify the two or three columns that address your A2 problem and note them — you will select only those columns in the Cleanup step. Pulling all two hundred columns through the workflow just because they exist is how you end up with an unreadable dataset.

artifactLines: Search 'CSV' or 'Spreadsheet' in the node panel — version-dependent; check n8n.io/docs.
Set the file path to the Kaggle CSV on your machine — use an absolute path.
Execute this node alone: look at the fields. Note the 2–3 columns that address the A2 problem.
Do NOT pull all columns through the workflow — select only the columns you need.
Rule: the CSV is a static file — you already own it; no internet required after download.
props.sparkLine: Absolute path. Two or three columns. Nothing else.

beats.narration_text: Three traps before you move to Episode 4. Trap one: three sources that are really one. If all three of your sources are RSS feeds from the same website, you have not demonstrated three kinds of data — you have demonstrated one kind three times. The grader is looking for genuinely different source types. At minimum: one feed, one API, one file. Trap two: pasting an API key into the URL or a node field. That key will appear in the exported workflow JSON. A submission with a visible API key is a security problem you have created and submitted. Use n8n Credentials — that is what they are for. Trap three: pulling 2,000 records because you can. The assignment calls for 50 to 200 clean records. 2,000 records is not better — it is a signal that you did not read the grading criteria. Set a page size or limit parameter on your API node before you run the full workflow.

artifactLines: Trap 1 — Three sources, same type: RSS + RSS + RSS is not 3 source types. Need feed + API + file.
Trap 2 — Key in node field: your key appears in the exported JSON. Use n8n Credentials instead.
Trap 3 — 2,000 records: 50–200 clean records is the target. Set a limit on your API node.
props.sparkLine: Three types. Key in Credentials. Limit your pull.

beats.narration_text: Verdict for Wire Up Your 3 Sources. The six-step skeleton: Start, Source 1, Source 2, Source 3, Cleanup, Save. Source 1 is an RSS Read node — feed URL only, no login. Source 2 is an HTTP Request node — API key stored in n8n Credentials, never in the URL. Source 3 is a CSV file — absolute path, only the columns that address the A2 problem. Execute each source node individually before wiring them together. Look at what comes out. If a node returns nothing or an error, fix it before moving to the Cleanup step. Episode 4 merges, deduplicates, normalizes dates, and saves — but that step only works if all three source nodes are returning clean output now.

props.artifactTitle: Build the Pipeline, Part 1: Wire Up Your 3 Sources
artifactLines: Skeleton: Start → Source 1 → Source 2 → Source 3 → Cleanup → Save.
Source 1 — RSS Read: feed URL, no login, execute alone and check fields.
Source 2 — HTTP Request: API key in Credentials (never in URL), execute alone.
Source 3 — CSV: absolute file path, only the 2–3 columns that address the problem.
Execute each node alone before wiring them together — fix errors before Cleanup.
3 source types required: feed + API + file. Three RSS feeds is not 3 types.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Paste this into Claude to get the right node type for each of your three sources. The prompt reads: I am building an n8n workflow with three data sources for this problem: [paste your A2 problem in one sentence]. My three sources are: [Source 1 name and URL or type], [Source 2 name and type — RSS, API, or CSV], [Source 3 name and type]. For each source, tell me which n8n node type to use, what fields to configure, and what the output will look like — specifically which fields I should keep for the A2 problem. Also flag any source that might need credentials rather than a public URL. That prompt is the scoping question before you wire anything. Claude tells you the node types; you verify them against n8n.io/docs before you drag anything onto the canvas. The docs are the source of truth.

props.segment: Build the Pipeline, Part 1: Wire Up Your 3 Sources
props.command: I'm building an n8n workflow for this problem: [A2 problem in one sentence]. My 3 sources: [Source 1], [Source 2], [Source 3]. For each: which n8n node type? What fields to configure? Which output fields to keep? Flag any source needing credentials.
props.runningText: verify Claude's node suggestions against n8n.io/docs before dragging anything onto the canvas…

beats.narration_text: Build the Pipeline, Part 1: Wire Up Your 3 Sources. Liam, in for Bear.
